query: DynamoDB operational complexity and managed options
answer: DynamoDB is fully managed and serverless, with operational complexity abstracted away. It offers automatic scaling and backup features. AWS manages all infrastructure and security policies.

results.title: AWS managed policies for Amazon DynamoDB
results.snippet: DynamoDB uses AWS managed policies to define a set of permissions the service needs to perform specific actions.

results.title: Amazon DynamoDB Features – NoSQL Key-Value Database – Amazon Web Services
results.snippet: Secondary indexes enhance DynamoDB's performance primarily by enabling efficient querying of data using attributes other than the table's primary key. You can enable PITR with a single click in the AWS Management Console or a single API call, and you can fully automate creation, retention, restoration, and deletion of backups via APIs. On-demand backup and restore allows you to create full backups of your DynamoDB tables’ data for data archiving, helping you meet your corporate and governmental regulatory requirements. For data that is infrequently accessed, DynamoDB Standard-IA table class reduces storage costs by 60% compared to existing Standard tables while delivering the same performance, durability, and scaling capabilities. For tables using provisioned capacity, DynamoDB provides auto scaling of throughput and storage based on your previously set capacity by monitoring the performance usage of your application. DynamoDB import and export capabilities help you easily move, transform, and copy DynamoDB table data acrpss applications, AWS accounts, and/or AWS regions with a few clicks in the AWS Management console or API calls.
